items.title: Wall Street Buys Into Musk’s SpaceX Vision
items.description: SpaceX is joining the Nasdaq 100 Index and analysts at big global brokers are recommending investors buy shares.

items.title: France Cuts 2026 GDP Forecast Due to Budget Delay, Iran Conflict
items.description: The French government is lowering its growth forecast for the year to 0.7% after a delayed budget and the Middle East conflict held back output, Finance Minister Roland Lescure told Le Parisien newspa

items.title: AI Productivity Gains Are Years Away, Deutsche Bank’s Reid Says
items.description: Artificial intelligence is likely to enhance productivity signficantly but any such impact on economies may still be years away, Deutsche Bank analyst Jim Reid said.

items.title: A Once-Great Copper Miner Faces a Reckoning Just as AI Power Demand Soars
items.description: Chile’s Codelco, facing scrutiny for a fatal accident and inflated production figures, is struggling under $25 billion of debt.

items.title: Chile revisa el futuro de Codelco en medio del boom mundial de la demanda de cobre
items.description: A medida que se dispara la demanda mundial de cobre, una de las mayores empresas mineras del mundo se encuentra en una situación precaria.

items.title: How Michaels Became an Unlikely Success
items.description: Arts and crafts store Michaels has gone from a "grandmother’s store" to an unlikely business success. Michelle Cheng breaks down how. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Goldman Says Earnings to Drive More Gains in Asset-Heavy Stocks
items.description: Capital-intensive companies are likely to report solid earnings this season, further outperforming peers that are more reliant on human or digital assets, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strateg

items.title: LNG Tanker Attack Threatens Qatar’s Efforts to Revive Hormuz Exports
items.description: A projectile hit the Al Rekayyat gas carrier on Tuesday, with the repercussions reverberating through the global gas market.

items.title: Extreme SK Hynix Stock Swings Add Wild Card to $28 Billion Deal
items.description: SK Hynix Inc.’s New York listing, currently on track to be the largest ever by a foreign company in the US, is facing an unusual source of uncertainty for a major offering: the wildest swings seen in

items.title: De Beers Makes Deep Diamond Price Cuts for Shrinking Buyers Club
items.description: De Beers has made some of the deepest ever cuts to its official diamond prices, potentially signaling an end to its years-long campaign to hold them well above the market rate.

items.title: China Regulator Is Keeping a Close Eye on Susquehanna Case
items.description: China’s securities watchdog said it is paying close attention to a legal case brought by Susquehanna International Group against alleged insider traders, after the US market maker said regulatory offi

items.title: Uzbekistan’s AKFA Picks Citi, JPM to Sound Out Bond Investors
items.description: AKFA Aluminium Group, one of Uzbekistan’s largest private companies, is set to meet international investors next week as it explores issuing its first ever bond.

items.title: China’s PBOC Buys Most Gold Since 2023 as Bullion Swings
items.description: China’s central bank bought more gold in June, extending the longest buying streak since at least 2015 and underscoring a commitment to diversifying its reserves despite volatility in bullion prices.

items.title: Italy Revives Dollar Bond Sales for First Time Since Pandemic
items.description: Italy is returning to the US dollar bond market for the first time since the pandemic, broadening its pool of foreign investors after the country’s improving finances won credit-rating upgrades.

items.title: Foreign Funds Return to India’s Banks After $12 Billion Selloff
items.description: Global investors returned to India’s financial stocks after a three-month selling streak, as the central bank’s recent measures to draw foreign capital ease funding constraints for lenders.

items.title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/7/2026
items.description: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews,

items.title: TalkTalk Wholesale Arm Is Said to Draw Interest From Telecel
items.description: Telecel Group is among bidders competing to acquire TalkTalk Telecom Group’s wholesale telecommunications business, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: China Touts Yuan as US Ups Strong Dollar: 3-Minutes MLIV
items.description: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Panetta Sees Fragile Outlook With Risks to Inflation, Growth
items.description: The European Central Bank is navigating an uncertain economic situation even as the US and Iran move closer to a lasting peace deal, according to Governing Council member Fabio Panetta.

items.title: Czech Policymaker Cautions on Services Prices as Inflation Slows
items.description: A Czech central banker signaled that tight monetary policy remains appropriate even after headline inflation slowed more than expected last month in the wake of the first interest-rate hike in four ye
